name: Kislevites
source: The New Mordheimer, https://mordheimer.net/docs/warbands/grade-1a-warbands/kislevites (fetched 2026-07-06). Grade 1a warband. Cited source: Town Cryer #16, revised in the Mordheim Annual 2002. Owner should cross-check against their own copy of that supplement/annual.

heroSlots.notes: 60 gc. Leader (6"). Inheritance: at warband creation only, may buy one item from the Kislevite Warrior equipment list at half price (an ancestral heirloom). If that specific item is ever lost (e.g. a "Robbed" Serious Injury result), it must be replaced at 150% of its normal price as soon as possible, or the Captain suffers -1 to all tests and to-hit rolls until it's replaced.
35 gc, 0-1. Cossack honorific meaning "Best Warrior", given to a veteran fighter.

35 gc, 0-1. Bear Handler: a warband including a Bear Tamer may recruit a Trained Bear henchman; the Bear auto-passes Stupidity tests while within 6" of the Tamer (even if the Tamer is Knocked Down/Stunned).

henchmenTypes.notes: 25 gc, any number. Gospodar tribesfolk, the bulk of Kislev's population.
30 gc, any number. Hate Chaos: subject to Hatred against any warband the group would apply the warband's Ancient Enemies rule to.

25 gc, 0-3. The only Kislevite troops trained to use handguns. Gun-Rest: a Streltsi armed with both halberd and handgun gets +1 to hit with the handgun as long as he didn't move that turn (even via a skill that would otherwise let him move and shoot).

125 gc, 0-1. Requires a Bear Tamer in the warband to recruit; may not take part in any battle the Bear Tamer doesn't, and if the warband has no Bear Tamer at all the Bear must stay behind at camp. No weapons/armour beyond claws and teeth. Trained: subject to Stupidity, but doesn't test while within 6" of the Bear Tamer; only ever uses the Bear Tamer's Leadership (within 6"), never the warband leader's. Fearsome: causes Fear. Bear Hug: if both of the Bear's attacks hit the same enemy in one round, may instead make a single Bear Hug attack — both players roll a D6 + their model's Strength; if the Bear's total is equal or higher, the enemy takes one automatic wound with no armour save, otherwise the enemy breaks free and takes no damage. Fiercely Loyal: as long as the Bear isn't taken Out of Action, the Bear Tamer ignores "Sold to the Pits", "Robbed", and "Captured" results on the Serious Injury table (treat as "Full Recovery" instead). Animal: never gains Experience. Large Target as defined in the shooting rules. statMaximums null: animals have no Human Maximum Profile.

exclusiveEquipment.name: Bear-Claw Necklace
exclusiveEquipment.rulesText: A necklace of bear claws (or teeth), sacred in Kislev and reputed to carry the bear's strength and ferocity. The wearer becomes subject to Frenzy. Variable price: 75 + 3D6 gc — the base 75 gc is what the app can prefill; roll and add 3D6 gc when actually buying it.

exclusiveEquipment.name: Vodka
exclusiveEquipment.restriction: Heroes only. One-use: given to the warband before a battle begins, then consumed.
exclusiveEquipment.rulesText: Every warrior in the warband gets +1 Leadership (max 10) for that game. Because of the alcohol, every warband member must also test Toughness before the game starts — on a failure, -1 Initiative for the whole game. Variable price: 35 + 2D6 gc — the base 35 gc is what the app can prefill; roll and add 2D6 gc when actually buying it.

specialRules: Kislev lies to the north-east of the Empire, a harsh land of endless winters under constant threat from Chaos and the raiding Norse — its people are famously resilient, warm-hearted among their own, and bitterly, unrelentingly hostile to anything tainted by Chaos. May hire: a Kislevite warband may hire the same Hired Swords as a Human Mercenary warband. Ancient Enemies: a Kislevite warband may never ally with any Chaos-aligned warband (Possessed, Beastmen, Skaven, Dark Elf, Chaos Dwarf, or any other warband the players judge sufficiently Chaotic).
